TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Tsou, story: TsouNr-pear-story-3

S.FORM: pan to moso la cihi ci mo no mo toa nasi ci eatatiskova eh mo alʉ na'no euho'a 'o isi toai ci nasi.
S.TRANSL: 有一個人採梨子。他採的那棵梨樹結實纍纍。
There was one...one man that gathered pears. The pear tree from which he gathered pears was well-fruited.

S.FORM: eh ne osi cu pemomoun'a toepʉnga eh micu asonʉ peeueusno khago 'o isi toai ci nasi.
S.TRANSL: 當他幾乎要（採）完的時候，已經採了幾乎兩個籃子。
When he almost finished (gathering pears), the pears that were gathered almost made two baskets...

S.FORM: eh mo 'ene 'enepio ci 'a'ausna 'o isi aiti.
S.TRANSL: 看到的情況有許多。
Several things/situations were seen/observed.

S.FORM: 'e mo coni pan to moso cihi ci mo sucaefi ci mo e'tʉ to moatʉ'nʉ ci eatatiskova mo sucaifi ho uk'a ci isi na'na u na'na hioa.
S.TRANSL: (首先)，有一個人牽著一隻羊經過，沒（什麼）事發生/（他）沒做（什麼）事。
(First), there was one man passing by pulling a goat, and nothing happened/was done (by him).

S.FORM: eh cu aomane pan to mo cihi ci oko mo moneo to jidensa ho maezo sucaefi.
S.TRANSL: 過了不久，有一個小孩也騎著腳踏車經過。
A while later/after that, a boy on a bike passed by, too.

S.FORM: 'e oko eni isi aiti ho mo na'no man'i 'e mo eon ta ceoa ci nasi.
S.TRANSL: 這個小孩看到許多梨子在地上。
This child saw plenty of pears which are on the ground.

S.FORM: mi cu tosvo ho isi iu'i'ia ho aiti.
S.TRANSL: (因此)，他停下來仔細看（那些梨子）。
(Therefore), he stopped to check carefully and see (the fruit).

S.FORM: eh mo asonʉ maica na to'tohʉngʉ ta oko mo mici meo'eoi.
S.TRANSL: 那小孩的想法或許是想要偷（那些梨子）
The thought of the child was probably like that he intended to steal (them).

S.FORM: at'inghi isi ake'a zou aiti na 'e mo noepe ta evi ho toa nasi ci i himnonu.
S.TRANSL: 但是他看了一下主人，他還在上面採梨子。
But he slightly saw the owner, who was staying above the tree picking pears.

S.FORM: eh mo ake'i ngoheʉngʉ nte aueu mici maeo ta mo c'o 'enepio ho hafa pkaako.
S.TRANSL: 他有一點害怕，想要先拿走一些就離開。
He was somehow/a little bit afraid, and intended to take only a small part of them first and escaped.

S.FORM: at'inghi isi ait 'e mot toa nasi ci eatatiskova ho a'a mo a'a isi na'na zou aiti na 'e 'e mo eon ta ceoa ci isi cu toai ci nasi.
S.TRANSL: 但是他發現採梨子的人沒在看那些採好放在地上的梨子。
Yet he noticed that the pear-picking man wasn't paying much attention to/looking carefully after the gathered pears on the ground.

S.FORM: ko'ko eno 'e oko mo atavei alʉ moezʉ cono khago 'o isi zou isi peo'eoza ho hafa eh mo'unu maita'e.
S.TRANSL: 因此，小孩最後直接偷走了一籃梨子，就那樣走了。
Therefore, the child finally stole one basket (of the pears) straightforward, took it away, and went in some direction. (It was something) like that (way).

S.FORM: a ne micu mi ta ceonʉ isi zou hafa na 'e 'e isi peo'eoza ci nasi.
S.TRANSL: 他走在路上，帶著他偷來的梨子。
When he was on his way, carrying the stolen pears,

S.FORM: ho aʉlʉ eupte'lʉ to mo cihi ci mo hmʉhmʉskʉ ci mamespingi.
S.TRANSL: 他碰巧遇見一個跟他一樣的女孩。
(he) happened to meet a girl who is similar with him.

S.FORM: na 'e hahocngʉ isi asona na'na huaca huseolʉa 'e mamespingi.
S.TRANSL: 那男孩或許在喜歡那女孩。
(and) probably liked the girl.

S.FORM: la a cono ceoa na 'e mo miuhu ci ci na ci nasi.
S.TRANSL: 梨子灑了一地。
The pears were spilt all over the place/ground.

S.FORM: inemio eh ine ntesi cu akoeva no ucia patmʉta.
S.TRANSL: 那時候，當他想要撿起梨子。
At that time, when he wanted to pick them up,

S.FORM: mo tueu ci mocmo ci 'o'oko ho ihe aiti 'e a'ausna ta mo smopcuku ci ha ci na ci oko.
S.TRANSL: 其他三個小孩，他們看見那個（男）孩跌下來。
Three children, the situation of the fallen child was seen by them.

S.FORM: ine mi nte c'u ina 'e mo tueu ci 'o'oko no hahocng hahocngʉ.
S.TRANSL: 當那三個男孩（與男孩）分開，
When the three boys departed ((each other) with the other child),

S.FORM: eh ihe aiti 'e mo eon ta ceoa ci ceopngu.
S.TRANSL: 他們看到一頂帽子在地上。
a hat was seen (by them) laying on the ground/road.

S.FORM: ihe cu patmʉta ho faeni ta himnonu ci oko.
S.TRANSL: (他們)撿起了帽子，還給主人那個小孩。
It was picked up (by them), and was returned to the owner (child).

S.FORM: eh ina 'e na 'e oko nenusi no mo aveoveoyu ho isi ho ihe to'usni.
S.TRANSL: 那孩子很高興他們幫他。
The child was somehow grateful that he was helped by them.

S.FORM: ihe cu isi cu pamacoconi ta nasi 'e mo tueu ci 'o'oko.
S.TRANSL: 因此，他給每個小孩一個梨子。
Therefore, the three children were each given a pear (by him).

S.FORM: inemio 'e mo tueu ci 'o'oko mi cu maita'e mo'usnu maica ai ai 'aa ihe cohivi 'e tehe 'e tehe sucaefiza ci 'e tehe mia ci ceonʉ.
S.TRANSL: 那三個小孩好像不知道要走哪條路。
The three children seemed to have no idea which road they were going to pass,

S.FORM: 'iama te sucaefi to mo toa nasi ci eatatiskova.
S.TRANSL: 剛好（他們）經過那個採梨子的人。
which happen to be the pass-by where the man gathered the pears.

S.FORM: inemio na 'e mo toa na 'e mo toa nasi ci eatatiskova osi cu zou cohivi ho ho ihe isi pa'eo'a 'o mo cono khago ci nasi.
S.TRANSL: 採梨子的人好像已經知道一籃梨子不見了。
Then, the pear-gathering man seemed to have learned that one basket of the pears was missing.

S.FORM: inemio isi cu 'aka ta'totohʉngva eainca ma sia na mo meo'eo'i.
S.TRANSL: 那時候他一直想是誰偷走了梨子。
(And) at that time he kept thinking of who might have stolen the pears.

S.FORM: aʉlʉ ne ne osi ta'totohʉngva mi cu sucaefi 'e mo tueu ci oko ho macoconi.
S.TRANSL: 就當他在想的時候，三個小孩剛好經過。
Just when he was thinking, the three children (happened to) passed by,

S.FORM: na'no kokakaebʉ ho mo mʉpʉtʉ ta ma ta ta nasi.
S.TRANSL: 而且每一位都很高興的手裡拿著一顆梨子。
and each was holding with great joy a pear in hand.

S.FORM: inemio na 'e ina 'e himnonu ta nasi isi cu s'a no o'te cohivi ho eainca no no ko no no koeusi eainca ma asonʉ hin'i co mo meo'eo'i.
S.TRANSL: 梨子的主人已經猜到「或許是他們偷走了梨子」。
The pear-owner must have realized/learned by guessing that they probably stole/have stolen (the pears).
